analysis: Telemetry indicates OpenMP barrier time and imbalance likely exacerbated by OS thread migration. The selected candidate is thread placement/affinity stabilization. This is primarily an execution-time optimization via OMP_PROC_BIND/OMP_PLACES; modifying code to force affinity is non-portable and can conflict with schedulers/runtimes. Safest implementation is to keep computation unchanged and optionally set default OpenMP affinity environment variables only if not already set by the user, guarded under _OPENMP. This preserves correctness and allows easy rollback by overriding env vars externally.
selected_candidate_target: OpenMP loop execution stability via thread placement/affinity
applied_changes_summary: Added conservative runtime defaults for OpenMP thread binding by setting OMP_PROC_BIND=true and OMP_PLACES=cores only when these environment variables are not already defined. No changes to the parallel loop or numerical computation.
correctness_risks: Some OpenMP runtimes/schedulers may ignore or reject affinity settings; performance may not improve or could degrade
Forcing defaults could interact with external launchers (e.g., srun) if users expect no in-program environment changes; mitigated by only setting when unset
expected_metrics: Reduced cpu_migrations/context_switches and lower omp_barrier_pct due to more stable thread placement
More consistent runtime and potentially improved scaling_efficiency on multi-core systems
